page: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory allocation of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data places the D64_v4 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. It achieves a PassMark CPU score of 46,322.49 and a multi-core stress-ng performance score of 59,622.57. Notably, it exhibits strong performance in the PassMark CPU extended instructions test, scoring 55,022.71, which falls into the top 25% category. This is supported by hardware-level support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI. Memory bandwidth is measured at 175.21 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 69.18 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

While the instance handles general-purpose tasks effectively, it exhibits performance limitations in specific workloads. For example, large language model text generation benchmarks show weak to poor performance, with the 135M model generating 40.31 tokens/sec and the 7B model generating 6.87 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may affect I/O-heavy applications. The D64_v4 is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio without requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most standard tasks, including a PassMark CPU score of 46,322.49 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 59,622.57. It demonstrates strong capabilities in extended instructions due to AVX-512 support, though it exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving where local storage is not a prerequisite.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced compute performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
256.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong performance in extended instructions utilizing AVX-512 vector extensions
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
